steps.title: Identify the relationship between cell size difference and nonhexagonal frequency
steps.content: The passage establishes that connecting sections of different-sized cells becomes harder as the size difference increases, forcing bees to use nonhexagonal cells to fill gaps. Therefore, fewer nonhexagonal cells indicate a smaller size difference between worker and drone cells.

steps.title: Match the data pattern to the correct conclusion
steps.content:
Choice A asserts that worker and drone cells are closer in size in western honeybee—exactly what the data confirms.

The western honeybee's nonhexagonal cell percentages (0.1–0.5%) are substantially lower than those in dwarf honeybee (2.1–2.6%) and black dwarf honeybee (2.4–2.5%).

The other three choices either misread this pattern or claim specifics the graph cannot support.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
